name: Azamara
description: Azamara is a boutique luxury cruise line specialising in destination-immersive travel. With just four intimate ships carrying fewer than 700 guests each, Azamara spends more time in port — including overnight stays and late-night departures — so travellers can experience each destination more deeply. Gratuities, house beverages, and an AzAmazing Evening shore event are all included.

name: Carnival Cruise Line
description: Carnival Cruise Line is the largest cruise brand in the world, known for its fun-focused atmosphere, vibrant entertainment, and affordable pricing. It is the flagship brand of Carnival Corporation & plc, operating 29 ships across a wide range of classes from the massive Excel-class giants to classic Fantasy-class vessels.

name: Celebrity Cruises
description: Celebrity Cruises is a premium cruise line offering a more upscale experience than mainstream lines. Known for its modern luxury ships, award-winning culinary programs, and destination-focused itineraries, Celebrity appeals to discerning travelers looking for a refined cruise experience.

name: Costa Cruises
description: Costa Cruises is Europe's leading cruise line, founded in Genoa in 1948 and now a wholly owned subsidiary of Carnival Corporation. All ships sail under the Italian flag, offering a Mediterranean lifestyle at sea with authentic Italian cuisine, passionate entertainment, and itineraries spanning the globe from the Mediterranean to Asia and South America.

name: Crystal Cruises
description: Crystal Cruises is an ultra-luxury cruise line founded in 1988 and relaunched in 2023 under A&K Travel Group, owner of Abercrombie & Kent. With only two intimate ships carrying 650–1,040 guests, Crystal offers a fully all-inclusive experience with butler service for every guest, unlimited beverages, all specialty dining, and shore excursions curated by Abercrombie & Kent — repeatedly voted the world's best cruise line.

name: Cunard Line
description: Cunard Line is the world's oldest and most celebrated ocean liner company, founded in 1840 by Sir Samuel Cunard. A British institution owned by Carnival Corporation, Cunard operates the only remaining scheduled transatlantic ocean liner service in the world aboard its flagship RMS Queen Mary 2. Famous for its White Star Service, formal evenings, afternoon tea, and the legendary three-tier Queens Grill dining tradition, Cunard is synonymous with timeless British elegance at sea.

name: Disney Cruise Line
description: Disney Cruise Line combines the magic of Disney storytelling with the excitement of a world-class cruise vacation. Known for its legendary service, Broadway-caliber entertainment, signature rotational dining, and exclusive private island destinations, Disney Cruise Line offers unforgettable experiences for families, couples, and fans of all ages.

name: Holland America Line
description: Holland America Line is one of the world's oldest and most prestigious cruise companies, founded in 1873 in Rotterdam. Now headquartered in Seattle and owned by Carnival Corporation, HAL is renowned for its classic elegance, exceptional service, and immersive destination experiences. The line is the leading cruise operator in Alaska, owning hotels and two railroads throughout the state. Its 11-ship fleet carries the names of legendary Dutch ocean liners, and the seventh Rotterdam serves as flagship.

name: MSC Cruises
description: MSC Cruises is the world's third largest cruise brand and the largest privately-owned cruise line. Founded by the Mediterranean Shipping Company, MSC combines European elegance with a multi-cultural experience, catering to passengers from more than 180 countries.

name: Norwegian Cruise Line
description: Norwegian Cruise Line (NCL) pioneered freestyle cruising, giving guests the freedom to dine when and where they want, with no set times or assigned seating. NCL is known for its innovative ships, specialty dining, and Broadway-style entertainment.

name: P&O Cruises
description: P&O Cruises is Britain's favourite and most iconic cruise line, with the oldest cruising heritage in the world dating to 1844. Operating exclusively from Southampton, P&O offers a quintessentially British experience at sea — from proper afternoon tea and Brodie's pubs to West End-calibre entertainment and Elemis spa treatments. The fleet ranges from intimate adults-preferred ships to the massive LNG-powered Iona and Arvia, the joint-largest ships ever built for the British market.

name: Ponant
description: Ponant is France's ultra-luxury expedition cruise line, founded in 1988 by officers of the French Merchant Navy. Operating exclusively under the French flag, Ponant combines Michelin-inspired French gastronomy with intimate expedition travel to the world's most remote and breathtaking destinations — from the Geographic North Pole aboard the icebreaker Le Commandant Charcot, to Antarctica, the Northwest Passage, and the Pacific islands. All voyages are fully all-inclusive with open bar.

name: Princess Cruises
description: Princess Cruises is one of the world's most iconic cruise lines, famously known as 'The Love Boat' after the hit 1970s–80s TV series set aboard the Pacific Princess. Founded in 1965 and now owned by Carnival Corporation, Princess operates 17 ships sailing worldwide itineraries. The line pioneered Movies Under the Stars poolside cinema and the MedallionClass wearable technology platform, which enables keyless cabin entry and on-demand service anywhere on the ship. Princess is particularly renowned for its Alaska sailings, its Royal-class ships, and its brand-new LNG-powered Sphere-class flagships.

name: Regent Seven Seas Cruises
description: Regent Seven Seas Cruises is the world's leading ultra-luxury, all-inclusive cruise line. Every voyage includes all-suite, all-balcony accommodations, unlimited shore excursions, all dining and premium beverages, flights, pre-cruise hotel stays, and gratuities — truly nothing is extra. The fleet of six intimate ships sails over 450 destinations worldwide, and Seven Seas Mariner holds the historic distinction of being the world's first all-suite, all-balcony ship. Regent is owned by Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings.

name: The Ritz-Carlton Yacht Collection
description: The Ritz-Carlton Yacht Collection is the world's most exclusive cruise brand, bringing the legendary Ritz-Carlton standard of personalised luxury hospitality to sea. The Collection operates three intimate superyachts — Evrima, Ilma, and Luminara — carrying between 298 and 456 guests with a nearly 1:1 crew-to-guest ratio. Every suite features a private terrace, Michelin-calibre dining spans multiple specialty restaurants, and the experience is deeply personalised from embarkation to disembarkation. Ilma became the world's first cruise ship to earn a Forbes Travel Guide Five-Star rating.

name: Royal Caribbean
description: Royal Caribbean International is a global cruise vacation company and the world's second largest cruise line by revenue. Known for its innovative ships and wide variety of onboard activities, Royal Caribbean caters to families, couples, and adventure seekers.

name: Seabourn
description: Seabourn is one of the world's foremost ultra-luxury cruise lines, founded in 1987 and a wholly owned subsidiary of Carnival Corporation since 1998. Operating intimate ships carrying fewer than 610 guests, Seabourn delivers a highly personalised, all-inclusive experience with open-seating dining, complimentary premium beverages, all gratuities, and complimentary water sports from each ship's retractable marina platform. The line operates two distinct product lines: the classic Encore-class ships sailing world-class luxury destinations, and the purpose-built Venture-class expedition ships — each equipped with two submarines and a fleet of Zodiacs — for remote voyages to Antarctica, the Arctic, and the world's most inaccessible coastlines.

name: Silversea
description: Silversea is an ultra-luxury all-inclusive cruise line and expedition travel brand founded in Monaco in 1994 and owned by Royal Caribbean Group since 2020. Silversea pioneered the all-inclusive luxury cruise fare, covering butler service for every guest, all fine wines and spirits, all specialty restaurants, gratuities, and Wi-Fi in a single price. The fleet of 12 ships spans two complementary products: the classic fleet sailing curated itineraries from the Mediterranean to Japan aboard intimate all-suite ships, and a dedicated expedition fleet venturing to the Antarctic Peninsula, the High Arctic, and the Galápagos Islands — where the purpose-built Silver Origin operates exclusively year-round.

name: Viking
description: Viking is a destination-focused cruise line offering ocean, river, and expedition voyages, founded by Torstein Hagen in St. Petersburg in 1997 as Viking River Cruises. Viking Ocean Cruises launched in 2015 and Viking Expeditions in 2022, building a portfolio of small and mid-size ships designed around a simple philosophy: no casinos, no children, no crowds. All ocean ships carry 930–998 guests, and every voyage prioritises cultural immersion and time in port over onboard entertainment. Wine and beer with meals, all specialty restaurants, Wi-Fi, gratuities, and one complimentary shore excursion per port are all included. The company listed on the NYSE in 2024.

name: Windstar Cruises
description: Windstar Cruises is a luxury small-ship line founded in 1984 by Karl Andrén as Windstar Sail Cruises, operating a fleet of intimate yachts carrying 148 to 342 guests. The line's three Wind-class ships — Wind Star, Wind Spirit, and Wind Surf — are among the world's only computer-controlled sailing cruise ships, unfurling Dacron sail from four or five towering masts to harness the wind. A second fleet of three Star Plus-class all-suite motor yachts — Star Breeze, Star Legend, and Star Pride, acquired from Seabourn in 2014–15 and dramatically stretched in 2021 — carries 312 guests each in all-outside-cabin luxury. The newest Star-class ships, Star Seeker (2025) and Star Explorer (2026), are purpose-built ice-strengthened luxury yachts introducing Windstar's first new-build generation. Across all eight ships, wine and beer with meals and water sports from the aft marina are complimentary, and Windstar's small size means calling at hidden harbours, private anchorages, and ports beyond the reach of larger cruise ships.
